channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Man City sign teenager Monga from Leicester for £10m
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Manchester City have completed the signing of Jeremy Monga from Leicester City.

item.title: Caverton reports pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion in 2025 as revenue declines
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Caverton Offshore Support Group Plc has released its 2025 audited results for the period ended December 31, 2025, reporting a pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion, representing a 74.15% reduction from the N53.67 billion loss recorded in 2024. The post Caverton reports pre-tax loss of N13.87 billion in 202

item.description: United States President Donald Trump has responded to reports of an alleged new Iranian plot to assassinate him... The post Trump reacts to reports of fresh Iranian plot to assassinate him appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Troops overpower terrorists, rescue four kidnap victims in Kebbi-Sokoto border
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of Sector 2, Operation FANSAN YAMMA, have rescued four kidnap victims after foiling a terrorist attack in Surimi, a border community between Kebbi and Sokoto states. The operation, carried out on Friday, followed credible intelligence indicating the presence of armed terrorists operating

item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The country has the highest rate of C-section births among the OECD's 38 nations, according to the last available data from 2023, with around 615 such procedures out of every 1,000 live births that year. item.title: Spanish royals mark minute of silence for wildfire victims
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
Spain's King Felipe VI, Queen Letizia and their two daughters observed a minute of silence Friday for the victims of a wildfire in the south of the country that claimed at least 11 lives.
"From this place, located some 150 kilometres (90 miles) from where this tragedy occurred, we want to convey o

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "What Nigerians expected yesterday was to see a commander-in-chief leading from the front. Following the successful rescue operation, the president ought to have immediately addressed the nation alongside his defence chiefs—not merely to announce the good news, but to brief Nigerians on the operatio

item.title: Over 900,000 Flee Their Homes In China Ahead Of Typhoon
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Typhoon Bavi is expected to make landfall in China early on Sunday, with a total of more than 900,000 people evacuated from their homes in the capital Beijing and the eastern metropolis of Wenzhou, city governments said. The post Over 900,000 Flee Their Homes In China Ahead Of Typhoon appeared first

item.title: 11 injured in overnight Russian missile and drone strikes on Kyiv
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: At least 11 people, including a child, were injured after Russia launched an overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv early Saturday. Our correspondent Gulliver Cragg tells us more.

item.title: Trump agrees to more Iran talks but insists truce is over
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Friday he had agreed to further negotiations with Iran, even as he repeated his assertion that the ceasefire between the long-time foes was over.

item.title: African results justify World Cup slots increase amid criticism
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: "Africa deserves fewer World Cup slots," he said, referring to the number of automatic qualifying places allocated to that continent rising from five to nine in an expanded 48-nation event. The post African results justify World Cup slots increase amid criticism appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: World Cup 2022: Spain edge Belgium, set up semi-final showdown with France
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Spain booked their place in the World Cup semi-finals on Friday with a dramatic 2-1 victory over Belgium, sealed by a late winner from Mikel Merino. La Roja will now face France on Tuesday for a place in the final.

item.title: Death toll in Venezuela quakes surpasses 4,000
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The death toll from Venezuela's devastating twin earthquakes has risen above 4,000, authorities said Friday, as families continued searching through the rubble for missing loved ones more than two weeks after the country's deadliest quake in over a century.

item.description: US president warns the US will decimate Iran amid alleged assassination plots as regional mediators try to save MoU.

item.title: How Abducted Oyo Schoolchildren, Teachers Were Rescued – Army
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: The victims were kidnapped on May 15, 2026, when armed assailants attacked their school in Oriire Local Government, triggering widespread concern and prompting a coordinated rescue mission involving multiple security agencies. item.title: ‘Talent speaks louder than tribe’: Rangers break silence on Moses Simon’s discrimination claims
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: In a detailed statement released on Friday and shared on the club’s official social media channels, the nine-time Nigeria Premier Football League champions insisted they have never operated, and do not operate, any policy that discriminates against players based on ethnicity, tribe, religion or plac

item.title: Top 20 Weekly Entertainment News Roundup For Latest Nigeria News
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Here is the latest New Telegraph’s top 20 weekly Entertainment news roundup, making headlines from Saturday, July 4th to Saturday, July 11th 2026. Burna Boy To Perform At World Cup Final Halftime Show Grammy Award-winning Nigerian Afrobeats singer, Damini Ogulu, popularly known as Burna Boy, has bee

item.title: Report: Sevilla strike ₦25bn deal with Venezia for Akor Adams
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Italian Serie A club Venezia have agreed to a deal with Sevilla for the transfer of Super Eagles striker Akor Adams, Soccernet.ng reports. ​Adams has been linked with several clubs this summer, but Venezia are the ones who have made concrete moves to sign him. ​They initially made a €10 million bid

item.title: Hey Jude: Bellingham's return to England indispensability
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Senior football correspondent Sami Mokbel charts Jude Bellingham's remarkable resurgence with England at the World Cup.

item.title: ‘Why foreign airlines rule Nigeria’s skies’
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dickson Omobola Chief Commercial Officer of United Nigeria Airlines, Mr Adedayo Olawuyi, has fingered the dominance of foreign carriers in Nigeria’s aviation market to the weakness of domestic airlines, saying the country lacks strong local and regional operators capable of competing effectively

item.title: OIS Services denies exit from Nigerian visa processing in the US
item.dc:creator: Technext
item.description: Online Integrated Solution has pushed back against reports that it has been permanently removed from handling Nigerian visa…

item.title: Death Toll In Venezuela Quakes Rises Past 4,000
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: At least 4,118 people were killed, and 16,740 were injured in the back-to-back June 24 quakes, which flattened entire districts in the coastal state of La Guaira, Venezuelan parliament chief Jorge Rodriguez wrote on Telegram. The post Death Toll In Venezuela Quakes Rises Past 4,000 appeared first on

item.title: France Should Fear Spain, Declares Confident Yamal
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Spain winger Lamine Yamal has insisted France, not Spain, should be the side feeling the pressure ahead of Tuesday’s FIFA World Cup semi-final. Yamal made the bold claim after producing a Player of the Match performance in Spain’s 2-1 quarter-final victory over Belgium. Spain will now face Kylian Mb

item.title: Wembanyama signs contract with Spurs for five years, $252m: Report
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Victor Wemnanyama, the NBA's tallest player, inked the third-largest rookie extension in history.

item.title: ‘If school, the one place built for my safety, became the site of my capture, where is safe?’
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: From the abduction of a former minister’s sister and her children in Ibadan to the mass kidnapping of pupils and teachers in Oriire Local Government Area of Oyo State, kidnapping-for-ransom has evolved into one of Nigeria’s most lucrative criminal enterprises. item.title: Portugal New Coach Jesus: Ronaldo Still Has A Place In My Squad
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: New Portugal head coach Jorge Jesus has insisted Cristiano Ronaldo will continue to be selected for the national team, provided he remains fit and available. Jesus made his position clear during his official unveiling on Friday, dismissing suggestions that the veteran forward could become a burden t
